description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c0m3t","text":"Campylobacter jejuni is a significant cause of foodborne illness in humans and animals. Rapid detection of animals shedding C. jejuni is a key step in limiting the scope of outbreaks and reducing the potential exposure of humans and other animals. Manual DNA extraction methods, although effective, can be labor-intensive and time-consuming. The utilization of automated or semi-automated methods can significantly reduce the time needed for DNA extraction and significantly increase the number of samples that can be processed in a given amount of time. This method was developed to provide a standard method for semi-automated DNA extraction from feces for the detection of C. jejuni in veterinary patients.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":20},{"style":"italic","offset":124,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":676,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"53ugt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ag899","text":"Validation data (in-house and by an independent laboratory via collaborative study such as Randomized Blinded Method Test) are available upon request. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
